product: VMware Cloud Foundation SDDC Manager REST API 9.1

create_token_pair.failure: non-201 raises VcfAuthError; the thrown message must not contain the password

refresh_access_token.request_body: the refresh token id serialized as a bare JSON string, e.g. "<uuid>" including the quotes

success.body: the new access token as a JSON string

refresh_access_token.failure: 404 means the refresh token is unknown/revoked; recovery is impossible

success.body: unpaginated array [{id, name, datastoreType, totalCapacityGB, freeCapacityGB, vmCount}]

auth_rules.attach: every data request carries 'Authorization: Bearer <current access token>'
auth_rules.refresh_trigger: only a 401 data answer triggers a refresh; the refresh happens once, then the original request is retried once; a second 401 raises VcfAuthError
auth_rules.no_refresh_on_403: a 403 raises VcfForbiddenError immediately with zero token traffic
auth_rules.server_errors: a 5xx raises VcfServerError carrying StatusCode, ErrorCode, Message and ReferenceToken from the Error envelope
auth_rules.error_types: the three exception types are distinct PowerShell classes named VcfAuthError, VcfForbiddenError and VcfServerError, each exposing StatusCode, ErrorCode and ReferenceToken properties

pagination_rules.request: pageNumber starts at 0 and increments by 1; pageSize is passed through verbatim on every page request
pagination_rules.termination: keep fetching until pageNumber reaches pageMetadata.totalPages; concatenate elements in page order
pagination_rules.empty: a filter that matches nothing yields an empty result, not an error

cluster.datastores: array of {id, name, datastoreType, totalCapacityGB, freeCapacityGB, vmCount}

export_shape.ordering: domains sorted by name (ordinal), clusters by name, hosts by fqdn, datastores by name; key order exactly as listed above
export_shape.stability: two exports of the same inventory are byte-identical even when the API returns collections in a different order
export_shape.source_of_clusters: clusters are fetched per domain via the domainId filter; datastores per cluster via the datastores subresource
